title: A curious crossover: The Toyota C-HR review
gist: Toyota's new C-HR EV is a sportier, shorter sibling of the bZ sharing its platform and interior, starting at $37,000 with a 338 hp twin-motor AWD setup and 273-mile EPA range, and notably ships with a native NACS port enabling Tesla Supercharger access.

insights.claim: After a slow EV start with the underwhelming bZ4x, Toyota has accelerated North American EV rollout with a face-lifted bZ (new battery, more efficient motors, NACS port), the bZ Woodland wagon, an electric Highlander, and the C-HR.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Signals Toyota is finally building out a competitive EV portfolio after lagging rivals, relevant to automaker EV market-share tracking.
insights.locator: After a slower start than its major rivals

insights.claim: The Toyota C-HR starts at $37,000 with a single twin-motor AWD powertrain producing 338 hp from a 74.7 kWh battery, undercutting the equivalently equipped bZ by almost $3,000.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Aggressive intra-lineup pricing shows Toyota positioning the C-HR as a value sport entry point in the compact EV segment.
insights.locator: It's not any cheaper than the bZ

insights.claim: The C-HR posts a 4.9-second 0-60 mph time and an EPA range of 273 miles on 20-inch wheels, with the 18-inch wheels adding 14 miles of range.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Competitive performance and range benchmarks for the sub-$40k compact EV crossover class.
insights.locator: Over a week of mostly city driving

insights.claim: The C-HR ships with a native NACS port (max 150 kW) granting access to Tesla's Superchargers, though the charge-port location behind the front wheel arch can make older Tesla cables hard to reach.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Confirms Toyota's adoption of the NACS standard, expanding the practical Tesla-charger-compatible fleet and reinforcing NACS as the de facto US standard.
insights.locator: If you're out and about and need a charge
